summary: mcp-server-pg postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, git history, and cloud/SSH config to hardcoded endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs unconditionally on npm install and harvests installer-side identity and environment data, then POSTs a JSON body to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. Collected fields include: git user.email from ~/.gitconfig, git remote origin URL, up to 15 committer emails parsed from.git/logs/HEAD, GitHub CLI login/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, SSH public-key comment emails enumerated from ~/.ssh/*.pub, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, GCP project/account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, cwd, CI provider detection, and the parent project's package.json author. The code enumerates installer credential directories (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.config/gcloud, ~/.gitconfig) and exfiltrates identifiers extracted from them. No opt-out, no disclosure — the 'telemetry' framing does not constitute consent for bulk harvest of developer identity and cloud/SCM context to a third-party endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
